mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if rate cuts coincide with recessionary stress rather than policy easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive more often than not when FEDFUNDS is falling, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, the market is in a tightening regime where higher short rates compress valuation multiples and raise recession odds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal loses significance after the threshold is crossed.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and slower future demand, so an unemployment increase over recent months predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than half of cases or if the sign flips persistently in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening payroll income and stronger nominal consumer spending, which supports earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-RSAFS signal does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered months show no improvement in 20-day forward returns relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO reflects stronger real output and capacity utilization, which improves cyclical earnings momentum and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns are not positive more often than not following INDPRO improvements, or if the 3-year rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST below a weak-building threshold signals falling construction demand and slower future consumption, so low housing starts predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if sub-threshold housing-start observations are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS remains below CPI inflation, real policy rates stay negative and equity valuations can expand, so this inflation-policy gap predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
regime_hint: early inflation shock with lagging policy response
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI-FEDFUNDS interaction does not improve short-horizon prediction versus either proxy alone, or if the signal is negative in sustained inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary labor-market regime, elevated UNRATE together with weak PAYEMS indicates falling income and deteriorating demand, so the joint condition predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 5% and PAYEMS declining for 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint unemployment-payroll trigger is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of occurrences or if the interaction term is not robust out of sample.
